commit.message:
Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit (#3584)

* Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit

The _NuGets, _NuGetsPreview, _Symbols, and _SymbolsPreview packages
exceeded the Azure DevOps 500MB size limit. Split each into:
  - .Managed (managed-only nupkgs, small)
  - .NativeAssets (native asset nupkgs, large)
  - Parent meta-package (depends on both sub-packages)

The download side (DownloadPackageAsync) already follows dependencies,
so consumers automatically get both sub-packages when requesting the
parent.

New nuspec templates:
  _NuGets.Managed.nuspec, _NuGets.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _NuGetsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _NuGetsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _Symbols.Managed.nuspec, _Symbols.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _SymbolsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _SymbolsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Managed/NativeAssets split with dynamic bin-packing

Instead of manually splitting _NuGets into .Managed and .NativeAssets
sub-packages, dynamically bin-pack all nupkgs into ~200 MB numbered
chunks (_NuGets.Dependencies.1, .2, .3, etc.). The parent meta-package
depends on all chunks.

This is self-balancing: as packages grow or shrink, the chunk count
adjusts automatically without any manual curation.

- Delete 8 .Managed/.NativeAssets nuspec files
- Add generic _Dependencies.nuspec template for chunks
- Rewrite nuget-special Cake target with first-fit decreasing bin-packing
- _NativeAssets handling (raw native binaries) is unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: convert merge-validations from agent to deterministic bash step

The merge-validations step was using an LLM agent (amplihack:core:architect)
for purely deterministic vote counting — no AI reasoning needed. Converting
to a bash step with inline Python:
- Eliminates agent startup overhead (~30-120s saved per cycle)
- Makes results deterministic (same inputs → same outputs)
- Reduces token consumption (no LLM tokens for counting)

Also fixes recurse-decision heredoc delimiters to use single-quoted form
(<<'DELIM' instead of <<DELIM) for consistency with verify-fixes and
accumulate-history — prevents shell expansion of adversarial codebase
content in validated_findings JSON (#3638).

commit.message:
fix: enforce worktree isolation in default-workflow (#3684, #3673) (#3693)

Fixes two worktree isolation bugs in default-workflow:

1. Worktree handoff (#3684): Checkpoint steps used silent fallback
   cd worktree 2>/dev/null || cd repo_path which caused changes to
   land in the main checkout when the worktree was inaccessible. Replaced
   with fail-loud validation that exits 1 if the worktree is missing.

2. Clean-worktree invariant (#3673): step-15-commit-push now validates
   git toplevel and branch name match the expected worktree before staging
   any changes. Prevents dirty state from leaking into unrelated commits.

Changes:
- Add step-04b-validate-worktree after worktree creation to verify
  directory exists, is a valid git worktree, and has correct branch
- Remove silent fallback pattern from checkpoint-after-implementation
  and checkpoint-after-review-feedback
- Add worktree + branch validation to step-15-commit-push with
  set -euo pipefail and HAS_REMOTE guard
- Fix step-14 version bump to cd into worktree, not repo_path
- Fix step-22b final-status to use worktree path
- Add 21 tests covering all invariants

Closes #3684
Closes #3673

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devr.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>

commit.message:
fix: add /claims endpoint and fix wizard Thymeleaf expression

Two bugs fixed:

1. Missing GET /debts/claims endpoint (debt-service)
   The creditor portal called /debt-service/api/v1/debts/claims for all
   claim list views (recovery, hearing, zero-balance, rejected). The endpoint
   did not exist; Spring routed 'claims' to @GetMapping(/{id}), causing
   Invalid UUID string: claims and opening the circuit breaker.

   Added:
   - CreditorClaimListItemDto: superset of ClaimListItemDto and
     HearingClaimListItemDto so both list views deserialize correctly
   - DebtRepository.findClaimsForCreditor(): filters by creditorOrgId,
     lifecycleState (mapped from status param), and zero-balance flag
   - DebtService.getClaimsForCreditor() interface + DebtServiceImpl impl
   - DebtController: GET /claims before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

   Status mapping: IN_RECOVERY->OVERDRAGET, HEARING->HOERING,
   REJECTED->TILBAGEKALDT, ZERO_BALANCE->outstandingBalance=0

2. Thymeleaf 500 on opret fordring wizard (creditor-portal)
   The step indicator in all four wizard templates used 'step < currentStep'
   in a th:classappend expression. The < character is invalid in HTML attribute
   values and caused a TemplateProcessingException. Fixed by replacing < with
   the Thymeleaf comparison alias 'lt' in all four step-*.html templates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redesign Gallery as interactive Blazor WASM showcase with 21 demos (#3578)

Replace 44 thin static samples with 21 rich interactive demos, each with
live controls (sliders, pickers, toggles, composable effect groups).

Project structure:
- Shared/ as net10.0 class library, Blazor/ as WASM host
- Solution converted to .slnx format
- Removed all legacy gallery platforms (UWP, WPF, Xamarin.Forms, etc.)

Architecture:
- SampleBase → CanvasSampleBase + DocumentSampleBase
- SliderControl/ToggleControl/PickerControl/GroupControl records
- DI-based SampleService with reflection discovery
- TreatWarningsAsErrors enabled, zero Gallery warnings

Demos: 2D/3D Transforms, Blend Modes, Blur, GIF Player, Gradient,
Image Decoder, Lottie Player, Nine-Patch, Noise Generator, Path Builder,
Path Effects Lab, Photo Lab, Shader Playground (5 SkSL presets),
Text Lab, Text on Path, Vector Art, Vertex Mesh, World Text,
PDF Composer (5-page with embedded viewer), Create XPS

Home page: card grid with search, category filter chips, dark mode,
Bootstrap Icons, type badges, entrance animations

Code quality: reviewed by 3 AI models, fixed memory leaks (SKPathEffect,
SKPaint, CTS disposal), null safety, per-frame allocations, async void →
async Task, bitmap caching, filter composition logic

commit.message:
fix: add missing /counts endpoint to DebtController

The creditor portal called GET /debt-service/api/v1/debts/counts
but the endpoint did not exist. Spring routed the request to
@GetMapping(/{id}) treating 'counts' as a UUID path variable,
causing MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException. This opened the
circuit breaker in the portal, showing zero counts on the dashboard.

Added:
- ClaimCountsDto in debt-service with inRecovery/inHearing/rejected/zeroBalance
- DebtRepository: countByCreditorAndLifecycleState + countZeroBalanceByCreditor
- DebtService.getClaimCounts(UUID) interface method + implementation
- DebtController: @GetMapping('/counts') declared before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

Also fixed start-demo.ps1: corrected the 'Opret fordring' URL from
/fordring/ny to /fordring/opret.

* Fix CFG predict_step receiving batch_idx as guidance_scale (#53)

* Initial plan

* Fix CFG predict_step signature conflict with PyTorch Lightning

Both CFG models had predict_step(self, batch, guidance_scale=None) which
caused PyTorch Lightning to pass batch_idx as guidance_scale, silently
overriding the guidance scale set on the model. This resulted in:
- Wrong guidance scales being used during evaluation
- Nearly identical scores across different guidance strengths
- Worse scores compared to non-CFG models

Fix: Changed signature to predict_step(self, batch, batch_idx=None) and
read guidance_scale from self.guidance_scale attribute instead.

* Add permutation_importance_drn.py for DRN/EMOS models with copula postprocessing

DRN/EMOS models output distribution parameters (mu, sigma) rather than
samples directly. This script extends permutation importance to handle
the extra copula step:

1. Load model + data, run baseline forward pass
2. Convert distributional params to forecast samples via ECC or GCA
3. Compute baseline energy score on the copula samples
4. For each channel: inject PermuteChannel → forward pass → copula → ES
5. Record (ES_permuted - ES_baseline) / ES_baseline

CLI: python -m genpp.eval.permutation_importance_drn \
       --run-path feik/genpp/m5y9kwlh --split val --copula ecc -v

Reuses helpers from copulas_eval.py (stack_predictions,
predictions_to_dataarray, do_ecc, do_gca) and permutation_importance.py
(_build_x_transform, _get_channel_info, parse_device, log_msg).

Co-authored-by: m-j-w-f <98650214+m-j-w-f@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add parallel launcher for DRN permutation importance

Discovers all input channels from cached metadata, launches one
permutation_importance_drn.py subprocess per channel in parallel.
Each subprocess writes to a unique temp CSV to avoid write collisions.
Results are merged into a single sorted output file once all finish.

CLI: python -m genpp.eval.launch_permutation_importance_drn \
       --run-path feik/genpp/m5y9kwlh --max-parallel 4 --device 0 -v

Features:
- --max-parallel flag to limit concurrent subprocesses
- --channels to subset channels (forwarded to each subprocess)
- Per-channel temp files prevent write collisions
- Merged CSV sorted by importance descending

* Fix NaN in spatial normalization: clamp variance and std in _compute_tensor_stats

Two sources of NaN in spatial mode:
1. Negative variance from E[x²]-E[x]² numerical cancellation → sqrt(negative) = NaN
2. Zero std at constant grid cells → division by zero = Inf/NaN in __getitem__

Fix: clamp variance to min=0 before sqrt, clamp std to min=1e-6 in spatial mode.
This prevents NaN in norm_stats which was propagating to all_vars_mean and all_vars_std.

Fix GitHub issue #3603: default-workflow step-04-setup-worktree fails on new repos without origin/main.  ## Problem default-workflow currently assumes that origin/main already exists when it reaches s (#3620)

* fix: agent resolver now handles 3-part refs (namespace:category:name) (#2856)

Recipes use 3-part agent references like 'amplihack:core:architect' but
the resolver only handled 2-part 'amplihack:architect'. The split on the
first colon left 'core:architect' as the name, which failed the safety
regex. All recipe steps using amplihack:core:* or amplihack:specialized:*
silently lost their agent system prompts.

Now parses 2-part and 3-part refs correctly, validates each segment
independently, and rejects 4+ parts.

* fix(quality-audit-cycle): prevent JSON from being interpreted as bash commands in verify-fixes (#2886)

* fix(quality-audit-cycle): prevent JSON agent output from being interpreted as bash commands in verify-fixes step

The verify-fixes step used python3 -c "..." with double-quoting. When the
template variables {{validated_findings}} and {{fix_results}} were expanded
into $VALIDATED and $FIX_RESULTS via bash variable substitution inside the
double-quoted python3 -c string, any double-quotes in the JSON (all JSON
keys/strings) terminated the outer bash double-quoted argument prematurely.
This caused bash to interpret JSON content (e.g. "json:", "cycle:", "validated:")
as shell commands, producing errors like:
  /bin/bash: line 17: json: command not found
  /bin/bash: line 19: cycle:: command not found

Fix: export the bash variables and use a single-quoted heredoc (<<'PYEOF')
so Python reads the JSON from os.environ instead of string interpolation.
This completely isolates the JSON content from bash string parsing.

Closes #2872

* fix: add safety valve to stop hook lock mode (#2874) (#2924)

* fix: add safety valve to stop hook lock mode to prevent infinite loops (#2874)

When lock mode is active and the agent has completed all work, the stop
hook blocks every stop attempt indefinitely — creating an infinite loop
of 100+ empty block cycles consuming API tokens with zero productive work.

Add a safety valve: after AMPLIHACK_MAX_LOCK_ITERATIONS (default 50)
consecutive lock blocks, auto-approve the stop and remove the lock file.
The user is notified via stderr and can re-enable with /amplihack:lock.

The threshold is configurable via environment variable for users who need
longer autonomous sessions.

Closes #2874

* fix: use run-script file for tmux eval pipeline to fix quoting issues (#2922) (#2939)

* fix: use run-script file for tmux eval pipeline to fix quoting issues (#2922)

The execute_remote_tmux method previously embedded the decoded prompt
directly in a tmux send-keys command:

    tmux send-keys -t SESSION "amplihack ... -p \"$PROMPT\"" C-m

When $PROMPT contained double quotes, dollar signs, backticks, or other
shell-special characters, the shell inside the tmux pane misinterpreted
the command, breaking end-to-end automation.

Fix: write a self-contained run script using a heredoc with a
single-quoted delimiter ('AMPLIHACK_RUN_EOF'), which prevents the outer
shell from expanding $(...) and $VARIABLE. Python f-string substitution
inserts literal base64 values before the shell processes the heredoc.
The script decodes both the prompt and API key from base64 at execution
time inside the tmux session, using properly-quoted "$PROMPT" to pass
the value as a single argument regardless of content.

Changes:
- Replace 4 fragile send-keys lines with a clean run-script approach in
  execute_remote_tmux() across all three executor.py copies
- Add 32 outside-in tests in tests/outside_in/test_eval_pipeline_tmux.py
  covering special character safety, heredoc quoting, API key encoding,
  tmux session creation, bash syntax validity, and regression prevention

All 19 existing unit tests and 32 new outside-in tests pass.

commit.message:
fix(recipes): wrap workstream_count in str() before .strip() (#3606) (#3637)

workstream_count is bash step output that serde_json may parse as
Value::Number(1) instead of Value::String("1"). Calling .strip()
directly on a Number fails with "method .strip() can only be called
on strings" in recipe-runner-rs versions without the #3589 coercion
fix. Wrapping in str() first guarantees a String value, making .strip()
safe regardless of recipe-runner version.

All 6 condition expressions in smart-orchestrator.yaml that compare
workstream_count to string literals are updated:
- execute-single-round-1-development
- execute-single-round-1-investigation
- create-workstreams-config
- launch-parallel-round-1
- execute-single-fallback-blocked-development
- execute-single-fallback-blocked-investigation

Verified: str(workstream_count).strip() works in both the Rust
condition evaluator and Python simpleeval fallback.

Fix item access type inference (#280)

* Infer element types for ItemAccessExpression

Previously myList{0} always returned Type.Any. Now inspects the collection
type and returns:
- DefinedList: union of element types
- DefinedTable: DefinedRecord matching table columns
- Plain List: AnyInstance (unknown elements)
- Plain Table: RecordInstance
- AnyUnion: recursively resolves from union members
- Optional access (?): same type but nullable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify nullable test assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix 3 type inference bugs: range nullable, error type, null coalescing

B3: Range expression with nullable operands now returns List instead of None.
    Nullability is a runtime concern, not a type error.

B4: ErrorRaisingExpression now returns None instead of Any.
    Error expressions never produce a value  they always throw.

B5: Null coalescing (x ?? y) now strips nullability from left operand
    in the result union, since ?? guarantees non-null from left side.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unnecessary Map copy in DefinedTable element type extraction

commit.message:
Complete Metal.jl GPU acceleration

- Fix run_gpu! loop to follow exact 16-step CPU integration order
- Fix recovery duration: use lognormal (matching CPU default) instead of exponential
- Fix dynamic network support: re-generate edges each step (was using static cached edges)
- Add SEIR GPU support (exposed→infected transition, recovery time setting)
- Fix SIS GPU: use ti_recovered-based recovery, add immunity waning + boost
- Add cache_edges option for static network optimization
- Optimize results: GPU-side sum() instead of full state download
- GPUDiseaseArrays extended with exposed/ti_exposed fields
- Add _sample_recovery_durations helper matching CPU distribution
- Correctness verified: r>0.999 for SIR, SIS, SEIR (30 seeds)
- Update GPU vignette with actual benchmarks and working examples
- Extension now 1,696 lines (was 1,385)

commit.message:
fix(person-registry): use JdbcTemplate in seeders to bypass Hibernate state detection

Replace JPA repository.save() with JdbcTemplate INSERT ... ON CONFLICT to avoid
Hibernate 6.x PropertyValueException when persisting entities with explicitly-set
UUIDs and @Version/@GeneratedValue annotations.

Also adds JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS to payment-service to disable grpc-netty native epoll
transport, resolving the SIGSEGV (exit code 139) on container startup.

commit.message:
feat(main): rework monorepo structure for loki-operators (#9)

* Rework monorepo structure for main branch

Restructure loki-operators from a simple merge of two repos to the
coordinated-workers monorepo pattern established by tempo-operators.

Changes:
- Add root-level .gitignore, .wokeignore, CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md, README.md
- Copy GitHub configs (.jira_sync_config.yaml, renovate.json5) to root .github/
- Copy issue/PR templates to root .github/
- Add CI workflows adapted from tempo-operators (promote, pull-request,
  quality-gates, release, tiobe-scan, update-libs); remove interface tests job
- Add product-level Terraform module from observability-stack

commit.message:
fix(health): align readiness status with OpenAPI schema

The readiness endpoint returned {"status":"not ready"} on failure,
but the HealthResponse schema only allows: ok, ready, unhealthy.

Change failure response from "not ready" to "unhealthy" to match:
- The OpenAPI schema enum definition
- The health endpoint's failure response pattern
- Expected behavior for health check standards

This ensures spec compliance and prevents validation errors in clients.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(openapi): add missing System tag definition

The /health and /readyz endpoints use tags: [System], but the System
tag was not defined in the top-level tags list.

Add System tag with description for health check and operational endpoints.

This fixes OpenAPI validation warnings and improves spec consistency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(openapi): correct server base URL and route paths

Address review feedback: OpenAPI spec had mismatched server URL.

Changes:
- Server base URL: http://localhost:8080/api/v1 → http://localhost:8080
- Update health endpoint: /healthz → /health (matches actual route)
- Add /api/v1 prefix to all versioned business API routes:
  - /encounters → /api/v1/encounters
  - /tasks → /api/v1/tasks
  - /consults → /api/v1/consults
  - etc.

This aligns with the actual routing structure:
- Root-level: /health, /readyz, /auth/*
- Versioned APIs: /api/v1/* (encounters, tasks, etc.)

The spec now correctly generates URLs matching the implementation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Update routes: /api/v1/encounters/{encounterId}

This ensures API implementation matches the OpenAPI specification.

commit.message:
Migrate 9 screens/components from hardcoded Colors to ThemeContext

Replace all `import { Colors } from '../constants/colors'` usages
with `import { useTheme } from '../contexts/ThemeContext'` in:
- components/DocumentCard.tsx
- app/merge.tsx
- app/files.tsx
- app/scan.tsx
- app/convert.tsx
- app/sign.tsx
- app/split.tsx
- app/compress.tsx
- app/viewer.tsx

Each component now calls `const { colors } = useTheme()` and applies
color values inline on JSX elements instead of in StyleSheet.create().
Color-related props (backgroundColor, color, borderColor, shadowColor)
are removed from static StyleSheet definitions and applied dynamically
via inline style arrays.

Special cases:
- compress.tsx: qualityOptions array moved inside component to use colors
- sign.tsx/viewer.tsx: HTML template strings updated to use colors.*
- scan.tsx: removed now-empty dangerButton/successButton style rules
